scores.metric: sandbox --disable all on the CLEAN (cheat-free) body = 15. The committed src matches only via 3 cheats. Loop body byte-identical; diffs are frame-size + prologue + 1 move.

sessions.lever: blocked-lane triage — measured clean pure-C floor (15, cheat_count 0), confirmed all-frame-gap

reviewer.evidence: candidate.c is the CLEAN pure-C body: cheat_asm_count = 0 (no register pins, no asm barrier, no unused_slack array, no volatile). Self-reviewed against the cheat catalog — all natural pointer-walk C. It is the honest floor, not a cheated match.

remaining_gap.summary: POLICY-GRADE, not a pure-C grind. Target reserves a 0x48 (72-byte) frame; the clean pure-C body needs only 0x28 (40 bytes). The 0x20 (32-byte) difference is UNUSED frame slack — nothing in the function semantics writes there. The committed src fabricates it with `s32 unused_slack[8]; (void)unused_slack;` (dead-vars-local-array, FORBIDDEN since 2026-05-31) + register pins + an INLINE_MOVE_ALIASING asm barrier (preserves a redundant `move s2,s0`). With those cheats the build matches; without them, sandbox-distance 15 (loop byte-identical, all diffs = frame size + prologue scheduling cascade + 1 move).
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: 0 regfix/asmfix, but the committed src body carries 3 IN-SOURCE cheats (unused_slack dead array, $16/$18 pins, move asm barrier). No legitimate pure-C construct produces 32 bytes of unused frame.

next_hypotheses: PROJECT-WIDE POLICY DECISION (the real path forward, per the park): either (1) re-sanction a NARROW dead-vars/frame-slack idiom for the specific case of a verified UNUSED-frame gap that no pure-C content fills (SOTN-grounded research like the volatile carve-out), OR (2) canonical-asm-authorize this cluster. Cluster sibling InitHiraRmd_80047FBC is in the IDENTICAL situation (dead-vars-local-array re-evaluation note).

Investigate WHY the original reserved 72 bytes: was there a local array/struct in the original Marionation source that the game uses but our reconstruction dropped? Check callers / the .data layout / Kengo (PS2 reuse) for a struct that AddTbpOfst writes to a stack temp. If a GENUINE local buffer exists, declaring it (used) legitimately produces the frame — that would be the clean close.
If neither: this stays INCOMPLETE pending the user's policy call — it is NOT closable by single-function grind and is NOT a GTE-leaf.

rejected_forms.form: committed src: unused_slack[8] + $16/$18 pins + move asm barrier
rejected_forms.reason: ALL THREE are catalogued cheats (dead-vars-local-array FORBIDDEN 2026-05-31; register pins; inline-move-aliasing asm injection). Not COMPLETED-C.
HONEST floor — 0 cheats, loop byte-identical, but 32-byte frame gap + prologue cascade remain. Cannot be lowered by pure-C (no legit way to make unused frame).
